title: This Raw Cheese Selection at Costco Will Blow Your Mind - Costco Haul

chunks.title: Introduction to Animal-Based Diet
chunks.summary: The speaker introduces their preference for an animal-based diet, which includes meat, organs, raw dairy, and organic fruits. They express a desire to showcase grocery items that fit this dietary preference, particularly at Costco.
chunks.transcript: you guys know that I'm a huge fan of animal-based eating what does that even mean it's meat organs we're not going to find any organs in Here Fruit honey raw dairy we're also not going to find any raw dairy in here but there is some decent grass-fed dairy in here there are some decent eggs I bet I can find some raw cheese in Costco fingers crossed and I know I can find some grass-fed beef and I know they have organic fruit so let's go let me show you what I would

chunks.title: Meat Selection at Costco
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses various meat options available at Costco, highlighting the difference between grass-fed and grain-fed beef. They emphasize the nutritional benefits of grass-fed beef, particularly in terms of healthy fats and vitamins.
chunks.transcript: buy in Costco all right guys Costco grocery haul we're going to start with meat they've actually got some fre pretty good meat at Costco I'm impressed by this you can see here they've got Angus beef patties here which are $666 a pound check this out you can get 100% grass-fed wagu beef or 100% grass-fed beef for $5 a pound so grass-fed beef is even cheaper than Angus grain-fed beef here what's the difference between grass-fed beef and grass-fed wagu beef didn't breed of cow this is going to be fattier but supposedly both both of these are 100% grass-fed you can see here on the nutrition facts that one patty of wagu has 38 G of fat let's compare that to the regular grass-fed beef regular grass-fed beef one patty has 30 gr of fat so I don't really worry about animal fats I think that they're healthy for humans if you're trying to lose weight thinking about fat in your diet certainly will help but in general I think that an 8020 mix for ground beef is great it's going to be flavorful there are many unique nutrients in the fat there are many fat soluble vitamins vitamin E vitamin K2 which has been associated with all kinds of cardiovascular benefits in beef patty fat there's going to be more of those in grass-fed beef versus grain-fed beef grass-fed beef fat and regular beef fat also contains steric acid which has been associated with satiety and leanness in humans so I think meat fat animal fat is healthy that's where Tallow comes from and I think these 100% grass-fed beef patties would be a great first choice

chunks.title: Cheese and Vitamin K2
chunks.summary: This segment covers cheese options at Costco, particularly focusing on raw cheeses and their benefits, especially the presence of vitamin K2. The speaker argues that certain dairy fats are beneficial and discusses misconceptions surrounding saturated fats.
chunks.transcript: for your Costco grocery haul so let me show you shopping for Cheese at Costco they're actually pretty good here for cheese they have four different raw cheeses that I'll show you in a second but I also want to point out that they have yurg so yurg is one of the cheeses that has the most vitamin K2 I think vitamin K2 is incredible for humans this one is pasteurized but ybg because of the propana bacteria in here has higher than average levels of K2 in terms of cheese high levels of K2 in the diet have been associated with decreased rates of all cause mortality decreased incidents of cardiovascular disease in the rdam study and multiple studies cheese is a health food especially the vitamin K2 content in cheese it's interesting because the mainstream medical establishment believes that saturated fat is bad for you but cheese milk butter all of these contain fat soluble vitamins like vitamin K2 which are clearly repeatedly significantly strongly associated with better cardiovascular disease outcomes so I think the mainstream is a little bit confused when it comes to saturated fat over here we've got guier you've got this gr Reserva iiro you've got peino Rano and you've got parmesano regano who knew that Costco had four different types of raw cheese that is pretty cool so raw cheese means it's made from milk that has not been pasteurized the majority of cheeses on this wall the milk has been pasteurized but for parmesano regano peino for the guer it's part of these European cheese is that the milk is not pasteurized probably preserves many of the important nutrients in there I think they taste better and I digest them better I like raw dairy in general versus pasteurized

chunks.title: Butter and Its Nutritional Value
chunks.summary: The speaker reviews different butter options at Costco, advocating for grass-fed varieties. They argue that butter contains important fat-soluble vitamins and debunk myths about the health risks associated with saturated fat.
chunks.transcript: Dairy whenever I can get it so lots of good raw cheese options here at Costco all right look at this butter Costco actually has a good selection here I don't know what happened here this is a travesty somebody put some spring mix Greens on the grassfed butter let's move those out of there not a fan of salad obviously you got organic salted butter you got regular butter you got grass-fed butter and you've got Cary gold butter and so this one says 95% grass-fed butter it's basically the same as KY gold this say product of New Zealand K gold is from Ireland ker gold got into trouble a little while ago for claiming it was grass-fed and not disclosing that they were fed some grains so you know I think that 95% grassfed butter is pretty good I prefer like a raw butter from an unpasteurized milk how do you make butter in the first place you let the cream separate and you just churn the butter so butter is incredibly healthy for humans butter contains many immunologically Active Components butter contains fat soluble vitamins like vitamin K2 butter is not something to be feared everyone these days seems to be hating on saturated fat this is an Antiquated notion in medicine there's so much evidence now that there's no connection between saturated fat and increased cardiovascular disease and I think that the fats in grassed butter help with satiety if you look at the studies dairy fat is associated with leanness both in adults and kids and dairy fat is associated with better outcomes in humans in many different clinical endpoints so do not fear dairy fat dairy fat also contains things like odd chain fatty acids C15 and c17 pentanoic acid heptanoic acid which are consistently associated with good health outcomes in humans so butter is a health food guys I would get this grass-fed

chunks.title: Oils and Their Qualities
chunks.summary: The speaker critiques oils available at Costco, particularly olive and avocado oils, explaining their preferences for organic and single-source oils. They mention the dangers of using oils that can be cut with seed oils and stress the importance of avoiding heating certain oils.
chunks.transcript: salted butter or the Cary gold is basically the same thing all right guys right across from the butter here you've got all the oils and I'm not going to get any of these I'm going to tell you why I don't like olive oil or avocado oil unless they're organic single Source cold pressed and present in glass so this one here is organic it's extra virgin it's in glass but it's not single Source it says produced from Italian grown olives look this is probably not a horrible olive oil but a lot of olive oils are actually cut with seed oils and unless it's from a single Source you just don't know I'm not trying to make it impossible for you guys to buy olive oil this is probably not a bad option at Costco but I would not use this one because I want it from single Source like I said a lot of avocado and olive oils are cut with seed oils same thing with this avocado oil I don't use this at all um I would use an olive oil if I were going to eat something like a salad the last time I ate a salad was maybe 15 12 13 years ago I don't e salads so I don't see much use of an extra virgin olive oil in my diet I just use animal fats butter Tallow ghee um very occasionally coconut oil when I have nothing else but almost never coconut oil sometimes I put coconut oil on my skin or if I'm going in a chlorinated pool I might put coconut oil on my skin to hopefully prevent the absorption of chlorine from the pool but if you're going to buy these guys don't heat them olive oil avocado oil are not meant to be heated they're better than seed oils in terms of linolic acid and polyan citrated fats

chunks.summary: In this segment, the speaker discusses the importance of choosing organic fruits and the potential pesticide risks associated with non-organic produce. They give examples of organic fruits available at Costco and offer tips on reducing pesticide exposure.

chunks.title: Vegetables and Their Health Impact
chunks.summary: The speaker shares their views on vegetables, particularly leafy greens, citing concerns over defense chemicals like oxalates that can contribute to health issues. They argue that a diet focused on animal products can provide sufficient nutrients without the risks associated with certain plants.

chunks.title: Fish Choices at Costco
chunks.summary: This segment covers the speaker's thoughts on fish options at Costco, noting the issues with farm-raised salmon and potential contaminants in all fish. They suggest that while fish can be part of the diet, it shouldn't be the primary protein source due to health risks.

chunks.title: Conclusion and Overall Quality of Costco
chunks.summary: The speaker concludes with a reflection on the quality of food available at Costco for an animal-based diet. They discuss the variety of nutritious options and encourage viewers that shopping there can align with health-based dietary choices.
chunks.transcript: eating beef protein you're not eating chicken protein there's a high likelihood you're going to have lots of heavy metals endocrine disrupting forever chemicals pfas microplastics which by the way are in every male testicle studied in a small study so we're swimming in microplastics guys fish has a lot of them quality matters I don't eat a lot of fish these days because of that but you do you so Costco is huge I could be in here for hours showing you guys every little thing but that was a good tour I I think we were able to look at a lot of good things and it showed you guys what kind of stuff I would buy there's actually a lot of good stuff in Costco I mean I know a lot of you guys shop here I think the majority of you guys shop here it gets a bad wrap but this place is pretty good they've got grass-fed beef they've got organic fruit they've got raw cheese they've got honey the honey is in plastic and it's we don't know where the sourcing is from it probably contains pesticides like glyphosate I'm super proud of this glyphosate free honey that we just released at lineage and I don't even think they sell Tallow at Costco and we also have a new Tallow from lineage which is the best Tallow you will ever try but we've also got good decent grass-fed butter here nothing raw and as we saw they've got decent yogurt from A2 cows it's pretty easy to eat animal based very healthfully at Costco there's actually pretty good amount of food here that's great and something that you could include on an animal based diet again I don't think everybody needs to eat an animal- based diet to the letter I just think it's a valuable place to start I try not to be dogmatic about it but eating this way helps a lot of people so hopefully that's helpful for you guys as a blueprint what kind of stuff I would buy in Costco and I'll see
